daily.title: DPIA voor Nextcloud Enterprise: SURF geeft positief advies, 15 risico's laag of opgelost
daily.summary:
SURF en Privacy Company voerden een overkoepelende DPIA uit op Nextcloud Enterprise (zelf-gehoste open-source samenwerkingssoftware). Aanvankelijk 15 gegevensbeschermingsrisico's, allemaal rond beheerders-/commerciële contactgegevens, ondersteunings- en optionele diagnostische gegevens en websitegegevens. Voor de eigenlijke content data van klanten werden geen risico's vastgesteld — Nextcloud host die niet en heeft er geen toegang toe. Met een voor de onderwijs- en onderzoekssector onderhandelde aangepaste verwerkersovereenkomst (DPA) plus mitigerende maatregelen zijn alle risico's laag of (worden) opgelost; SURF geeft een positief advies onder de voorwaarden van die DPA.

De aangepaste DPA beperkt Nextcloud als verwerker tot 4 doeleinden en machtigt het als zelfstandig verwerkingsverantwoordelijke voor 7 verenigbare doeleinden. Instellingen houden volledige zeggenschap over doeleinden en subverwerkers, bewaartermijnen zijn tot het strikt noodzakelijke beperkt en alleen noodzakelijke cookies worden geplaatst. Kiest men voor Nextcloud Office op Euro-Office, dan blijft verwerking volledig binnen de EU; de DPIA zelf is getest met de oudere Collabora-versie, waarbij supportverzoeken in het VK verwerkt konden worden.

Soevereiniteits-hoek: anders dan VS-leveranciers geeft Nextcloud een 'harde garantie' nooit persoonsgegevens aan autoriteiten buiten de EU te verstrekken — de contractueel-technische kant van dezelfde CLOUD Act/FISA-zorg uit de eerdere Solvinity/Kyndryl-DigiD-notitie. De DPIA bevat ook een positieve beveiligingsbeoordeling (Nextcloud behaalde de Franse ANSSI CSPN-certificering) met concrete aanbevelingen: zelf hosten of via SURF, deling van gegevens met Nextcloud minimaliseren, en interne auditlogs niet misbruiken voor medewerker-/studentmonitoring. Gedateerd 16 juli 2026, als inhaal-item gefileerd.

items.title: AP consulteert over lijst DPIA-uitzonderingen voor werknemersgegevens
items.summary: The AP opened a consultation on a draft list of DPIA exceptions for employee-data processing — operations that would not require a full DPIA. Responses open until 10 Aug 2026. Based on a secondary report; the AP's own consultation page could not be retrieved this run, so verify before citing to clients.
items.action: Review the draft exceptions and consider a consultation response before 10 Aug; a 'when do you NOT need a DPIA for employee data' client memo is a natural follow-up once the list is final.

items.title: AP Rapportage datalekken 2025: meldingen stijgen, accountovernames bijna verdrievoudigd, AI vergroot risico
items.summary: AP's annual breach report (8 Jul 2026): 39,407 breaches reported in 2025 (vs 37,839 in 2024), of which 2,428 from cyberattacks. Account-takeover breaches nearly tripled (607 → 1,742). The AP warns AI is making phishing faster, more convincing and larger in scale, and urges organisations to fix their digital security now.
items.action: Use the numbers (takeover tripling, AI-driven phishing warning) as an outreach hook: offer a breach-readiness / MFA & detection quick-scan and update incident-response training with the 2025 stats.

items.title: Code of Practice on marking/labelling AI-generated content — FINAL published
items.summary: The Commission published the final, voluntary Code of Practice on marking/labelling AI-generated content, operationalising the Art. 50 transparency obligations that apply from 2 Aug 2026 (machine-readable marking, deepfake/AI-text labelling, disclosure when users interact with AI; Annex 1 provides EU icons). The AI Office encourages signing by 22 Jul 2026 to be listed as an initial signatory; systems on the market before 2 Aug get a grace period to 2 Dec 2026.
items.action: Alert genAI-deploying clients to the 2 Aug obligations and the 22 Jul signing window; build a reusable Art. 50-readiness checklist (marking, deepfake labelling, chatbot disclosure, EU icons).

items.title: Odido datalek: ACM sluit aan bij AP/RDI-onderzoeken
items.summary: The ACM announced (14 Jul 2026) it is joining the AP/RDI investigations into Odido's major February data breach. Division of labour: RDI on technical security (Telecommunicatiewet), AP on personal-data security and retention periods, ACM on consumer protection. Three regulators on one breach — a procedural development, not yet a concluded enforcement action.
items.action: Use as a client conversation starter on data-retention hygiene — a breach exposes every record kept past its retention limit. Offer a retention-schedule quick-scan.

items.title: Cyberbeveiligingswet in force 15 August 2026 — corrects earlier "1 July" entry
items.summary: The Eerste Kamer approved the Cyberbeveiligingswet (NIS2 implementation) and the Wwke (CER implementation) on 7 Jul 2026; both enter into force 15 Aug 2026 — the planned 1 July date was missed. From 15 August, roughly 8,000 Dutch organisations face NCSC registration, risk-management measures and incident-reporting duties. Corrects this radar's earlier '1 July' entry.
items.action: Correct any client material citing 1 July; August outreach: 'Cbw is now law — are you registered with NCSC and is your incident-reporting chain ready before 15 August?'

items.title: EDPB calls for legal basis for cross-regulatory information sharing
items.summary: At its High-Level Meeting in Dublin (16-17 Jul 2026), the EDPB called on the Commission to propose a legal basis letting regulators with different competences — data protection, DSA/DMA, AI Act and other digital-rulebook supervisors — exchange enforcement-relevant information, including confidential information. A policy statement, not yet legislation, signalling coordinated cross-regime enforcement.
items.action: Talking point for compliance-strategy sessions: treat GDPR, AI Act and sector supervisors as one coordinated enforcement surface. Watch for a Commission proposal, possibly via the broader Digital Omnibus.

items.title: Consultation: expanded bank access to the Handelsregister — Privacy First critical
items.summary: An internetconsultatie is running on a bill amending the Handelsregisterwet 2007 to give banks and other private parties expanded access to register data. Privacy First filed a critical response (16 Jul 2026), arguing it walks back recent privacy gains (non-public residential addresses since 2022, business-address shielding) in favour of KYC/Wwft access. Fits the pattern of the separate BRP-access-for-banks proposal. Close date not confirmed this run.
items.action: Pull the primary consultation page for the close date and scope; content idea pairing the BRP and Handelsregister bank-access proposals and the GDPR homework they carry.

items.title: Geert Potjewijd becomes AP chair on 1 August 2026
items.summary: Geert Potjewijd takes office as bestuursvoorzitter of the AP on 1 Aug 2026, succeeding Aleid Wolfsen after ten years (appointed for five years; announced 13 May 2026). He spent 27 years at De Brauw, co-heading the Data Protection & Cybersecurity practice; commentary notes he often defended big tech, prompting speculation about a shift in the AP's enforcement posture.
items.action: August client-conversation starter on what the leadership change may mean for pending AP matters and the AI/transparency enforcement agenda; watch his first public statements after 1 August.

items.title: DPC fines X Corp €550m over MoPub advertising data (UNVERIFIED — secondary sources only)
items.summary: Ireland's DPC has reportedly concluded its inquiry into X Corp with a €550m fine for unlawful processing of personal data for advertising via the MoPub ad network, including special-category inferences (political opinions, sexual orientation), per aggregator/press reports (early-to-mid July). UNVERIFIED — no DPC press release or published decision found on dataprotection.ie this run; verify before any client use. Distinct from the separate X/Grok inquiry.
items.action: Once primary-verified, strong outreach angle: an 'inferred special-category data in ad targeting' audit under Art. 9. Watch dataprotection.ie for the decision and breakdown.

items.title: EU Action Plan on Cybersecurity and Artificial Intelligence
items.summary: The Commission presented an Action Plan on Cybersecurity and AI (7 Jul 2026): promoting safe use of advanced AI (stronger pre-market model evaluation), strengthening EU cyber resilience (ENISA 'European Blueprint', a secure testing platform for critical sectors), and expanding EU AI-for-cybersecurity capabilities (Grand Challenge; Critical Open Source Resilience Campaign, pilot Q4 2026). A policy plan with no new obligations; complements the AI Act, CRA, NIS2, DORA and the Cyber Solidarity Act.
items.action: Background/context slide for AI-governance and NIS2/Cbw engagements: the EU is operationalising 'AI × cybersecurity' as one policy surface. No client to-do yet; watch for the ENISA blueprint and the Q4 2026 open-source pilot.

items.title: AP advies: 11 voorwaarden voor het verwerken van persoonsgegevens bij fraudesignalen
items.summary: The AP published advice (~17 Jul 2026) setting eleven conditions for processing personal data on the basis of fraud signals: purpose and necessity substantiated up front (a hunch that a signal 'may be useful' is not enough), no more data than necessary, continuous assessment of each signal's reliability, no longer retention than necessary, data-subject notification in principle within one month (postponable for ongoing investigations), and extra restrictions for special-category/criminal data. Aimed at executive bodies and supervisors, but reads across to any fraud-detection or integrity controller.
items.action: Turn the eleven conditions into a client checklist / DPIA input for fraud-signal processing; outreach hook to clients with fraud or integrity-monitoring flows. Flag the one-month notification duty and special-category restrictions as most commonly missed.
